extractions.problem: Edge UE experience degradation and load imbalance in virtual radio access networks with cooperative transmit points.
extractions.method: UE-centric metric-based network partitioning into virtual TP sets with soft UE-TP association for load balancing. Numerical simulations.
extractions.finding: The proposed technique eliminates edge UE experience and soft association facilitates load-balancing among TPs.
extractions.baseline: conventional VTP partitioning without soft association

extractions.problem: Need for a computational framework that enables cell biologists to construct spatial models incorporating realistic experimental geometries.
extractions.method: Modular computational framework (Virtual Cell) with Java web interface, 3D spatial modeling, numerical solvers, and geometry from microscope images.
extractions.finding: The Virtual Cell facilitates communication between cell biologists and mathematical biologists and aids interpretation and planning of experiments.

extractions.problem: Understanding the initiation and propagation of ectopic excitation in cardiac ventricular cells and tissues.
extractions.method: Computational models of human ventricular cells simulating after-depolarizations and up/down regulation of membrane conductance systems in homogeneous/heterogeneous tissues.
extractions.finding: Ectopic foci require sufficient size and coupling to drive surrounding tissue; propagation depends on tissue heterogeneity and anisotropy.
extractions.dataset: human ventricular cell models (isotropic, anisotropic, orthotropic tissues)

extractions.problem: Design of virtual manufacturing cells using optimization methods.
extractions.method: Mathematical programming approach.

extractions.problem: The mechanism behind age-related decline in primary CD8+ T cell responses and the role of virtual memory (TVM) cells.
extractions.method: Proliferation assays in aged mice and humans, adoptive transfer experiments, and molecular profiling of TVM cells.
extractions.finding: TVM cells become senescent with aging and are poorly proliferative in aged mice and humans, while conventional naive T cells retain proliferative capacity. This marks the first observation of senescence in an antigenically naive T cell population.
extractions.dataset: aged mice and human CD8+ T cells
extractions.baseline: conventional naive T cells (TN), young TVM cells
extractions.result: TVM poorly proliferative in aged; TN retain capacity

extractions.problem: Fairness of resource management for V2X multicast communication in user-centric virtual cell ultra-dense networks with vehicle mobility.
extractions.method: Dynamic user-centric virtual cell (DUVC) scheme with mobile tracking and approximation algorithm for max-min-fair resource management.
extractions.finding: The proposed DUVC scheme is suitable for V2X communication and the algorithm outperforms three existing algorithms in fairness.
extractions.result: outperforms all three baselines in fairness

extractions.problem: Joint dynamic AP clustering and precoding in user-centric virtual cell networks under time-varying channel conditions.
extractions.method: Two-stage approach: discrete particle swarm optimization for AP clustering exploiting channel correlation, then distributed WMMSE-based precoding.
extractions.finding: The DAPC-Pre algorithm converges fast and significantly outperforms benchmark algorithms in sum spectral efficiency under dynamic environments.
extractions.dataset: simulated user-centric virtual cell network with random walk mobility
extractions.baseline: benchmark AP clustering and precoding algorithms
extractions.result: significantly outperforms benchmarks in sum SE

extractions.problem: Frequent handover failures and ping-pong effects in ultra-dense networks due to dense small cell deployment.
extractions.method: Virtual cell-based mobility enhancement with optimized handover parameters (TTT, A3 Offset) and VC forming/reforming based on user direction and speed.
extractions.finding: System HOF rate is minimized by optimizing handover parameters; PP rate decreases more than 50% for moderate-speed users compared to LTE.
extractions.metric: handover failure (HOF) rate, ping-pong (PP) rate, SINR
extractions.result: PP rate decrease >50% vs LTE

extractions.problem: Distributed resource allocation (sub-channel, modulation, power control) in multi-cell OFDMA networks where pure non-cooperative games yield poor outcomes.
extractions.method: Non-cooperative game with a virtual referee that monitors and improves competition outcomes by adjusting rates or restricting sub-channel access.
extractions.finding: The virtual referee reduces transmitted power by 80% vs fixed channel assignment and 25% vs iterative water-filling, with 10% rate improvement and up to 40% power reduction in multi-cell severe interference.
extractions.dataset: simulated multi-cell OFDMA network (two-cell and multi-cell)
extractions.result: 80% power reduction vs fixed, 25% vs water-filling, 10% rate improvement

extractions.problem: Production planning in virtual cells of reconfigurable manufacturing systems.
extractions.method: Genetic algorithm.

extractions.problem: Building generative statistical models of subcellular organization from microscopy images to learn possible phenotypes and inform simulations.
extractions.method: Automated computational methods for constructing generative statistical models of cellular/nuclear shapes and subcellular structure arrangement from image data.
extractions.finding: These models enable consistent phenotype analysis, discrimination, and can provide realistic geometry and initial protein locations for simulations of cellular processes.

extractions.problem: No standard mechanism exists for using BioPAX pathway data from Pathway Commons to annotate and build quantitative mathematical models.
extractions.method: New Virtual Cell module for pathway data retrieval, visualization, and automatic creation of executable network models from qualitative pathway connections.
extractions.finding: The module enables direct integration of Pathway Commons data into Virtual Cell for quantitative modeling.

extractions.problem: Understanding when, where, and how virtual memory (VM) CD8 T cells are generated and maintained during normal development.
extractions.method: Analysis of VM cell populations in IL-4R-deficient animals, tracking VM appearance in periphery vs thymus, and assessment of basal proliferation and long-term maintenance.
extractions.finding: VM population size is reduced in IL-4R-deficient animals. VM cells appear first in the periphery (not thymus), show basal proliferation, and are durably maintained, including during unrelated infections.
extractions.dataset: mouse CD8 T cells (IL-4R-deficient and normal mice)
extractions.result: reduced VM in IL-4R-deficient; VM appears first in periphery

extractions.problem: Inefficient resource utilization and pseudo congestion in TD-LTE systems where users in overlapping coverage cannot use resources from multiple base stations.
extractions.method: Virtual cell concept enabling users to utilize resources from multiple base stations with adaptive sub-frame allocation and cross-slot interference management.
extractions.finding: Virtual cells resolve pseudo congestion, enable customized user-specific resource utilization, and demonstrate performance enhancement over conventional TD-LTE.
extractions.result: performance enhancement vs conventional TD-LTE

extractions.problem: Forming virtual manufacturing cells that integrate machine grouping and layout design.
extractions.method: Four-phase approach: primary cell formation by operation range division, cell redesign for minimizing intercell travels, sub-cell decomposition, and forced decomposition for multiple designs.
extractions.finding: The methodology effectively forms virtual cells with minimized intercell travels and addresses the machine duplication problem.

extractions.problem: Frequent handover failures and high signaling overhead due to dense small cell deployment in ultra-dense networks.
extractions.method: Virtual cell design with local anchor cell for intra/inter-VC handover, virtual PCI, virtual reference signals, and split bearers.
extractions.finding: Spectral efficiency gains up to 11% and handover failure reduction by more than 50% compared to traditional LTE.
extractions.result: 11% SE gain, >50% HOF reduction vs LTE

extractions.problem: The antigen-specific function of virtual memory (VM) CD8 T cells is poorly characterized and their contribution to pathogen responses is unclear.
extractions.method: Functional characterization of polyclonal VM cells including IFN-γ synthesis, differentiation phenotype, and Listeria monocytogenes infection control experiments.
extractions.finding: VM cells have unique functional properties distinct from naïve or antigen-primed memory cells: poor TCR-induced IFN-γ but efficient Listeria control, bridging innate and adaptive immunity.
extractions.dataset: mouse polyclonal VM CD8 T cells, Listeria monocytogenes infection model
extractions.baseline: naïve and antigen-primed conventional memory CD8 T cells
extractions.result: poor IFN-γ but efficient Listeria control

extractions.problem: Building AI-powered virtual cells using data-driven and closed-loop learning approaches.
Scheduling algorithm design for virtual cells.
Enhancing QoE in TD-LTE pico-cell hotspots where pseudo-congestion degrades user experience.
extractions.method: SDN-based framework with virtual cells enabling multi-base-station resource utilization, QoE-centric MOS monitoring, and centralized TD-LTE frame optimization.
extractions.finding: The framework leads to notable improvements in MOS, end-to-end delay, and packet loss rate compared to static and adaptive frame reconfiguration approaches.
extractions.metric: Mean Opinion Score (MOS), end-to-end delay, packet loss rate
extractions.baseline: static and adaptive frame reconfiguration in TD-LTE picocells
extractions.result: notable improvement in MOS, delay, and packet loss

extractions.problem: Functional job shops and classical cellular manufacturing systems perform poorly in volatile manufacturing environments; virtual cells need better layout strategies.
extractions.method: Novel capability-based distributed layout approach with simulated annealing heuristic algorithm for virtual cellular manufacturing.
extractions.finding: Capability-based distributed layout outperforms functional layouts for forming virtual manufacturing cells in dynamic environments.
extractions.metric: layout performance for virtual cell formation
extractions.result: distributed layout superior to functional layout

extractions.problem: Resource allocation and energy efficiency in IoT low-power wide-area networks (LoRaWAN) with massive heterogeneous devices.
extractions.method: Multilayer virtual cell-based spatial time division multiple access (STDMA) scheme with flexible spreading factor allocation and power consumption optimization.
extractions.finding: The proposed method simplifies interference calculation, enables flexible cell radius adjustment, and significantly improves data rate and energy efficiency over conventional methods.
extractions.result: significant improvement vs conventional method

extractions.problem: The mechanism behind the dramatic increase of memory phenotype CD8 T cells with aging is unknown; the prevailing hypothesis of lifelong antigenic stimulation lacks evidence.
extractions.method: Phenotypic analysis and functional studies in CD4-deficient, CCR5-deficient, and CXCR3-deficient aged mice to distinguish antigen-driven vs virtual memory CD8 T cell accumulation.
extractions.finding: Central memory CD8 T cells in aged mice are virtual memory cells that develop without antigenic stimulation, independent of CD4 T cells, CCR5, and CXCR3.
extractions.dataset: aged mouse CD8 T cells (CD4-KO, CCR5-KO, CXCR3-KO)
extractions.metric: central memory CD8 T cell proportion
extractions.result: accumulation independent of CD4, CCR5, CXCR3

extractions.problem: Handoff decision in virtual cell layout-based tactical communications systems requires considering multiple criteria beyond signal strength.
extractions.method: Multicriteria handoff decision algorithm (MDA) using fuzzy inference system processing signal strength, soft capacity, relative direction and speed of access points.
extractions.finding: MDA with lowest membership threshold achieves minimum handoffs; MDA with highest threshold achieves minimum call blocking rate, both at acceptable levels.
extractions.metric: number of handoffs, call blocking rate (GoS)
extractions.result: minimum handoffs or minimum blocking rate depending on threshold

extractions.problem: Severe inter-virtual-cell interference in ultra-dense networks with user-centric virtual cell paradigm due to irregular dense deployment.
extractions.method: Interference graph-based virtual cell merging with zero-forcing beamforming within clusters and multi-virtual-cell MMSE precoding for inter-cluster interference mitigation.
extractions.finding: The proposed approach achieves the average user rate of the overlapping-based grouping scheme with smaller virtual cell size and reduced signal processing complexity.
extractions.dataset: simulated UDN with uniformly distributed users and base stations
extractions.metric: average user rate, virtual cell size, complexity
extractions.baseline: grouping scheme based on virtual cell overlapping
extractions.result: comparable rate with smaller cell size and reduced complexity

extractions.problem: Channel state information acquisition overhead for virtual MIMO in multi-cell distributed antenna systems is overwhelming.
extractions.method: Coordinated transmissions using only large-scale CSIT with joint input covariance optimization via successive approximation and saddle-point theory.
extractions.finding: The proposed scheme converges quickly, yields significant performance gains over existing schemes, and achieves near-globally-optimal performance under diagonal input covariance constraint.
extractions.result: significant gains, near-globally-optimal with diagonal constraint

extractions.problem: Complex interference in ultra-dense networks and the need for load-aware virtual cell design to avoid cell overload.
extractions.method: Load-aware user-centric virtual cell scheme with optimal radius finding and adaptive joint communication activation threshold based on virtual cell load.
extractions.finding: The proposed scheme avoids cell overload and achieves a reasonable balance of resources and performance compared to no-load-aware case.
extractions.result: avoids overload, balanced resources vs no-load-aware

extractions.problem: Complexity in automotive assembly systems with cooperating robots leads to long ramp-up times and delayed time-to-market.
extractions.method: Virtual commissioning (VC) techniques applied to an industrial robotic cell with cooperating robots, including complete virtual validation workflow.
extractions.finding: VC enables more efficient handling of assembly system complexity and significant reduction in ramp-up time.
extractions.dataset: industrial robotic assembly cell with cooperating robots

extractions.problem: Complex engineered microfluidic systems are needed for compartmentalized nucleic acid amplification, limiting accessibility.
extractions.method: Hydrogel-based virtual microfluidics with in-gel digital multiple displacement amplification (dMDA) applied to purified DNA, bacterial cells, and human microbiome samples.
extractions.finding: Virtual microfluidics enables whole-genome sequencing of single-cell MDA products with excellent coverage uniformity and markedly reduced chimerism compared to liquid MDA.
extractions.dataset: purified DNA, cultured bacterial cells, human microbiome samples
extractions.result: excellent coverage uniformity, reduced chimerism vs liquid MDA

extractions.problem: User-centric elastic resource management across multiple eNBs in TD-LTE systems.
extractions.method: SDN architecture for TD-LTE virtual cells.

extractions.problem: Integrative bioinformatics approaches toward building virtual cell models.
Whether reactive oxygen species (ROS) are relevant to apoptosis given observations of apoptosis occurring in virtual anaerobiosis.
extractions.method: Measurement of hydrogen peroxide production by cells and mitochondrial preparations under aerobic and virtually anaerobic conditions, with ceramide analog treatment.
extractions.finding: Cells and mitochondria produce similar levels of H2O2 under aerobic and anaerobic conditions; these levels are relevant to ceramide-induced apoptosis acting at complex III center o.
extractions.result: similar H2O2 levels in aerobic and anaerobic

extractions.problem: Whether intraepithelial T lymphocytes (IELs) of the small intestine can mature extrathymically or require thymic influence.
extractions.method: Review and discussion of evidence regarding IEL maturation and repertoire selection in the presence and absence of thymus.
extractions.finding: IEL precursors are present in the thymus and their differentiation pathway depends on thymic presence, challenging claims of fully thymus-independent intestinal T-cell development.

extractions.problem: Interference from co-locating virtual cells in ultra-low latency vehicular networks where open-loop transmissions prevent beamforming/interference alignment.
extractions.method: Multiuser detection (MUD) including reduced-computation ML-MUD and generalized linear MMSE equalizer with multi-antenna techniques.
extractions.finding: Modulation sensitivity of ML-MUD BER can be eased by multi-antenna; proposed reduced-computation ML-MUD and generalized LMMSE achieve satisfactory BER with lower complexity.
extractions.result: satisfactory BER with reduced complexity

extractions.problem: Extending user-centric virtual cells to V2X broadcast groups while maximizing served hotspots with minimum energy and guaranteed reliability.
extractions.method: Three-stage optimization: admission control, transmission weight selection, and power control for multi-user-centric virtual cells serving vehicle hotspots.
extractions.finding: The proposed solution increases network capacity, reliability, and power efficiency compared to baselines.
extractions.result: increased capacity, reliability, and power efficiency

extractions.problem: Scientific 3D data has been limited to 2D visualization, reducing understanding of complex cellular structures.
extractions.method: Virtual reality immersion using consumer VR headsets (Oculus Rift, HTC Vive) with 3D cell models built from serial block-face scanning electron microscope (SBEM) imaging data.
extractions.finding: VR immersion significantly improves students' understanding of cellular processes and enables new modes of scientific exploration and public engagement.
extractions.result: significant improvement in student understanding

extractions.problem: Rigorous estimation of forces on rolling leukocytes in blood is difficult due to the particulate, non-Newtonian nature of blood with red blood cells.
extractions.method: Lattice-Boltzmann simulation of leukocyte rolling in a virtual blood vessel with explicit red blood cell interactions at varying hematocrit.
extractions.finding: Red blood cells augment leukocyte binding via normal force and promote rolling via tangential force/torque; close envelopment by RBCs is necessary for significant force changes.
extractions.metric: normal force, tangential force, torque on leukocytes
extractions.result: RBC forces sufficient to increase leukocyte binding

extractions.problem: Load imbalance in downlink LTE-A systems requiring effective self-optimizing load balancing.
extractions.method: Virtual cell-breathing based load balancing (VCBLB) adjusting virtual transmission power to handover UEs from heavily to lightly loaded cells.
extractions.finding: VCBLB is feasible and more efficient than traditional load balancing schemes for LTE-A self-optimizing networks.
extractions.result: more efficient than traditional schemes

extractions.problem: Overlapped user-centric virtual cells in UDN cause severe inter-VC interference; merging all overlapped VCs has high complexity overhead.
extractions.method: Joint overlapped VC merging based on overlapped TP ratio threshold and iterative beamforming via generalized WMMSE approach.
extractions.finding: The scheme balances system complexity and inter-VC interference and achieves significant throughput gain.
extractions.result: significant system throughput gain with balanced complexity

extractions.problem: Worker assignment into virtual manufacturing cells under dual resource constraints in dynamic production environments.
extractions.method: Artificial neural networks (multilayer perceptron feed-forward) trained on simulated worker assignment datasets for virtual cellular manufacturing.
extractions.finding: The ANN-based approach effectively models complex worker assignment relationships and performs well under two cell configurations across different time periods.

extractions.problem: Maximizing energy efficiency in user-centric virtual cell networks with beamforming and cluster formation under rate and power constraints.
extractions.method: WMMSE-based transformation with Lagrangian dual method yielding closed-form solutions, novel threshold-based AP clustering, and K-dimension grid search.
extractions.finding: The algorithm converges fast, is robust, and greatly outperforms benchmarks in energy efficiency and data rate.
extractions.dataset: simulated user-centric network with multi-antenna APs
extractions.result: greatly outperforms benchmarks in EE and data rate

extractions.problem: Virtual resource allocation in small-cell networks with full-duplex self-backhauls is a mixed combinatorial non-convex optimization problem with high complexity.
extractions.method: Problem decomposition into two subproblems solved by ADMM-based distributed algorithm (convexified) and infrastructure provider-level convex optimization.
extractions.finding: The distributed scheme effectively handles virtualized resource allocation with reduced signaling overhead and scalability compared to centralized approaches.
extractions.dataset: simulated virtualized small-cell network with FD self-backhaul

extractions.problem: Multi-robot manufacturing cell design and commissioning requires flexibility that current physical prototyping approaches cannot efficiently provide.
extractions.method: Digital twin methodology with immersive VR interface for process design, virtual testbed validation, operator training, and real-time monitoring.
extractions.finding: The methodology enables validated assembly process automation design before physical implementation, applicable to operator training and feasibility studies.
extractions.dataset: assembly manufacturing use case with collaborative robots

extractions.problem: Synchronizing control between real and virtual industrial robots and enabling universal control through VR.
extractions.method: Digital twin synchronization model using Unity3D game engine for VR-based control and simulation of industrial robotic cells.
extractions.finding: The developed synchronization model enables real-time control of industrial robots through VR and can be modified for a wide range of industrial equipment.

extractions.problem: Achieving low-latency V2V communication in highly dynamic vehicular networks underlaying V2I networks.
extractions.method: Time dynamic optimization with Lyapunov optimization theory to jointly optimize spectrum resources and constrain network switching rate, converted to deterministic problem.
extractions.finding: The proposed scheme approaches the best tradeoff between latency performance and network switching rate.
extractions.result: approaches best latency-switching tradeoff
